title: Vertical Sleeve Gastrectomy Procedure in Mice
author: Teri Stevenson
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.zcjf2un

steps.title: All animals must be singly-housed, given...
steps.instruction: All animals must be singly-housed, given Ensure 12 hours before surgery and have all bedding removed.

steps.title: Preoperative pain medications should be administered
steps.instruction: Preoperative pain medications should be administered: a. Ketoprofen (5 mg/kg) b. Saline is given at the end of surgery and a second dose is given 24 hours later.

steps.title: Place mouse on surgical board/surgical field...
steps.instruction: Place mouse on surgical board/surgical field over a warm water circulating blanket and immobilize gently.

steps.title: Begin the procedure by making a...
steps.instruction: Begin the procedure by making a midline laparotomy incision with sharp surgical scissors. Be sure to stay on the linea alba and away from the rectus abdominus muscles.

steps.title: Using cotton swabs, gently sweep the...
steps.instruction: Using cotton swabs, gently sweep the intestinal contents until the stomach is located.

steps.title: Gently cut away all connective tissue...
steps.instruction: Gently cut away all connective tissue around the stomach and ligate the vessel attached to the greater curvature of the stomach using cautery pen or 7-0 PDS II.

steps.title: Place ligatures on all stomach vessels using 7-0 PDS II
steps.instruction: Place ligatures on all stomach vessels using 7-0 PDS II.

steps.title: Using 6-0 monofilament suture a continuous line below your ligatures
steps.instruction: Using 6-0 monofilament suture a continuous line below your ligatures.

steps.title: Remove greater curvature of the stomach...
steps.instruction: Remove greater curvature of the stomach by cutting below ligated vessels and monofilament line of suture.

steps.title: Close stomach using 7-0 PDS II continuous pattern
steps.instruction: Close stomach using 7-0 PDS II continuous pattern.

steps.title: Next, close the abdomen in a simple, two-layer, interrupted fashion
steps.instruction: Next, close the abdomen in a simple, two-layer, interrupted fashion. The first interrupted layer should be a simple, interrupted muscle layer with 6-0 monocryl suture. Using good technique suture the connective tissue and not the muscle proper. The second layer is a skin layer also done in a simple, interrupted fashion with 7-0 Prolene suture.

steps.title: All mice receive 1
steps.instruction: All mice receive 1.0 ml of warmed, sterile saline following the procedure before being placed in the recovery cages.

steps.title: All mouse cages are kept partially...
steps.instruction: All mouse cages are kept partially on a veterinary-approved heating pad for 5 to 7 days postoperatively.

steps.title: Mice will remain on an Ensure...
steps.instruction: Mice will remain on an Ensure diet for 24 hours post-surgery with no bedding in the cage.

steps.title: Mice are monitored until recovered from...
steps.instruction: Mice are monitored until recovered from the procedure, which typically takes 7-14 days depending on the procedure. General behavior (i.e. bright/alert/responsive vs. depressed/obtunded) is monitored.

steps.title: Pain medication is administered per protocol
steps.instruction: Pain medication is administered per protocol: ketoprofen 5 mg/kg post-op and again at 24 hours postoperative.

steps.title: Additional pain medication may be needed depending on postoperative recovery
steps.instruction: Additional pain medication may be needed depending on postoperative recovery.
